name: respawn-social-backend
//: Deno tasks. Run with `deno task <name>`. The permission flags are Deno's security model: a script can only touch the network/env/filesystem you explicitly grant.
//nodeModulesDir: Use a real node_modules dir populated from package.json. Lets us keep bare npm imports (e.g. `from "hono"`) and our existing package.json deps.
//unstable: sloppy-imports lets us keep Node-style `./foo.js` import specifiers that actually point at `./foo.ts` (Deno is normally strict about extensions). This is what allows the existing import style to run unchanged.
//compilerOptions: Deno supplies its own lib types; we add DOM for fetch/URL/Request types used by Hono, and keep strictness on.
//exclude: Keep Deno's own tooling from scanning build/vendor dirs.
